metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students develop proficiency in manual and computerized accounting practices, learning to prepare financial statements, manage payroll systems, and apply GST regulations. This course bridges foundational accounting principles with practical business applications.
metadata.general_description: An applied course in manual and computerized accounting systems, financial statements, and tax applications.

big_ideas.statement: Accounting systems provide a structured framework for tracking, analyzing, and communicating financial information.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding how accounting records transactions and generates reports enables informed business decision-making and financial planning.

big_ideas.statement: Financial statements reveal the financial health and performance of a business or organization.
big_ideas.elaboration: Balance sheets, income statements, and cash flow statements provide stakeholders with critical insights into profitability, solvency, and liquidity.

big_ideas.statement: Regulatory compliance and ethical accounting practices form the foundation of trust in financial reporting.
big_ideas.elaboration: Proper application of GST, payroll regulations, and accounting standards ensures legal compliance and maintains stakeholder confidence.

big_ideas.statement: Technology transforms accounting operations, improving accuracy, efficiency, and accessibility of financial data.
big_ideas.elaboration: Computerized systems automate routine tasks and enable real-time financial analysis and reporting.

competencies.raw: Design accounting systems that meet organizational needs and compliance requirements
i_can_statements: I can analyze business operations to design appropriate chart of accounts structures
I can develop accounting procedures for specific business types and sizes
I can plan financial record systems that ensure data integrity and accessibility
I can design audit trails and internal controls to prevent fraud and errors

competencies.raw: Create financial documents and reports that communicate accounting information clearly
i_can_statements: I can prepare formatted financial statements suitable for different audiences
I can design accounting worksheets and reconciliation schedules
I can develop financial analysis reports with interpretive commentary
I can create supporting schedules that substantiate main financial figures

competencies.raw: Plan accounting solutions that optimize efficiency and decision-making
i_can_statements: I can recommend accounting software features for specific business needs
I can develop accounting workflows that minimize manual data entry
I can design reporting timelines that support management decision-making
I can create budget and forecasting frameworks for financial planning

competencies.raw: Evaluate accounting methods and systems for effectiveness and improvement
i_can_statements: I can assess accounting system performance against organizational objectives
I can identify inefficiencies in accounting processes and propose solutions
I can compare accounting methods and select the most appropriate approach
I can evaluate the impact of accounting decisions on financial outcomes

competencies.raw: Execute accounting transactions using proper journal entries and posting procedures
i_can_statements: I can record complex transactions in general journals with appropriate accounts
I can post entries accurately from journals to general ledgers
I can handle subsidiary ledgers for accounts receivable and payable
I can reconcile accounts and identify and correct errors

competencies.raw: Prepare financial statements using appropriate accounting principles and methods
i_can_statements: I can prepare trial balances and adjusted trial balances
I can create income statements, balance sheets, and statements of changes in equity
I can apply adjusting entries for accruals, deferrals, and depreciation
I can analyze and interpret financial statement relationships and ratios

competencies.raw: Manage payroll systems including calculations, deductions, and regulatory compliance
i_can_statements: I can calculate gross pay, deductions, and net pay for various pay scenarios
I can prepare payroll records and maintain employee files appropriately
I can manage payroll tax remittances and statutory filings
I can interpret employment standards and pension plan obligations

competencies.raw: Apply GST and accounting software competencies in practical business contexts
i_can_statements: I can calculate and account for GST in sales and purchases
I can file GST returns and track input tax credits
I can operate computerized accounting software efficiently
I can manage data entry, backups, and system security protocols

competencies.raw: Use accounting software and digital tools to record and analyze financial data
i_can_statements: I can set up chart of accounts and company profiles in accounting software
I can enter transactions and generate reports from computerized systems
I can use spreadsheet formulas for accounting calculations and analysis
I can export and integrate accounting data with other business systems

competencies.raw: Apply technology for financial analysis, forecasting, and decision support
i_can_statements: I can create financial models for scenario analysis and budgeting
I can use data visualization to present accounting information effectively
I can perform ratio analysis and variance analysis using technology
I can generate custom reports and dashboards for management review

competencies.raw: Implement data security and integrity measures in accounting systems
i_can_statements: I can establish user access controls and authentication protocols
I can perform regular system backups and disaster recovery procedures
I can maintain audit logs and track system changes for compliance
I can protect sensitive financial information and maintain confidentiality

competencies.raw: Evaluate emerging accounting technologies and their business applications
i_can_statements: I can assess cloud-based accounting solutions for business use
I can evaluate automation opportunities in accounting processes
I can understand the role of artificial intelligence in financial analysis
I can identify cybersecurity risks and implement protective measures

areas.name: Manual Accounting Fundamentals
topics: The accounting cycle and double-entry bookkeeping principles
Journalizing and posting transactions to general and subsidiary ledgers
Preparing trial balances and identifying accounting errors
Understanding accounts: assets, liabilities, equity, revenue, and expenses
Account reconciliation methods and error correction procedures
Internal controls and fraud prevention in manual systems

areas.name: Financial Statement Preparation
topics: Adjusting entries: accruals, deferrals, and depreciation calculations
Preparing income statements for various business types
Creating balance sheets and statements of changes in equity
Cash flow statement preparation and analysis
Financial statement analysis including ratio calculations
Interpreting financial information for business decision-making

areas.name: Payroll and Tax Applications
topics: Payroll calculations: gross pay, deductions, and net pay
Income tax withholding and CPP/EI contributions
Employee files and payroll record maintenance
Payroll tax remittances and regulatory filings
GST (Goods and Services Tax) registration and calculations
GST input tax credits, returns, and compliance requirements

areas.name: Computerized Accounting Systems
topics: Accounting software setup and company profile configuration
Electronic transaction entry and automated posting
Report generation and customization features
System backups, security, and data recovery protocols
Integration with spreadsheets and other business applications
Transitioning from manual to computerized accounting systems
